codeReview.summary: The implementation correctly connects the workspace to the real API. It successfully sets up Next.js middleware for JWT checking, adds a login proxy, implements fallback conditional MSW initialization for offline behavior, and modifies the @emerald/data-access client to dynamically inject auth tokens and handle offline fallback using relative paths.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker implemented a dual-cookie strategy in the login proxy (setting both an httpOnly cookie for the Next.js server-side middleware and a non-httpOnly cookie for the client-side data-access client). This enables the frontend to make authenticated absolute-URL API calls while keeping the main session token secure from generic XSS.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: apps/workspace/src/app/api/auth/login/route.ts sets WORKSPACE_AUTH_CLIENT_COOKIE_NAME with httpOnly: false, and packages/data-access/src/index.ts reads it via readAuthCookieToken().

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker created explicit .js re-export files (e.g. document.js re-exporting document.ts) inside packages/contracts/src. This serves as a workaround for Next.js and NodeNext ESM resolution issues when consuming sibling TypeScript packages in this monorepo structure.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Commit includes multiple added files in packages/contracts/src/*.js containing 'export * from "./[name].ts";'.

summary: Feature successfully integrates workspace auth, connects the client to the real API, and provides a robust offline fallback to MSW.
